full_text: Walk us through like from when you started to your audience what your path looks like. Yeah so I started posting consistently on Twitter mid last year so maybe like eight months ago at that time I had maybe 2000 followers on Twitter and I hadn't posted on Twitter for eight yearsow because before that I felt like Twitter was a lot of clickbait I just didn't open Twitter for a long time then I realized all the AI stuff is happening on Twitter and I want to be part of the AI conversation I was like I have to be on Twitter.So the first thing I did which is very important is to before you even start posting start curating your timeline, what I mean by that is because I had neglected my account for a number of years, my account was full of topics I didn't care about anymore or people who are just trying to get clicks and the content quality was not very good and I noticed that if I want to actually learn about AI, I should follow builders, not influencers, like not pure influencers, but people who are builders and have an audience.At the same time, like people like Andudre Kazzi, for example, people who actually share tips and sharing what they're building, the learning journeys, etc cetera, so I started following people who are founders who are working in the research labs who were putting out good stuff as like developers, just people who are sharing genuinely useful things instead of purely trying to get clicks and I actively unfollowed a lot of the people who I didn't want to see their content on my timeline and when I saw that type of content like,Oh ChadGBT is dead or like breaking I would proactively click not interested so telling the algorithm don't don't give me that anymore after maybe two weeks of like intentional curation my time became very clean right now my Twitter is basically an AI learning platform is literally all like super useful like product launches or pros or like new technologies is coming out is very very useful when I see that kind of stuff on my timeline.It also inspires me to create similar content it also gives me new ideas and then when you engage with that kind of people and genuinely engage with them, they're also more likely to engage back and then they're more likely to see your content on their timeline going forward and that's how you grow it took maybe three to four months of consistent posting before seeing any result and I think in the beginning it's important to not just post but also engage with other people but I don't use those automated AI engagement bots.Actually genuinely engaged because people can tell if you're being authentic or not and then when you're actually being authentic they return your authenticity if someone likes or engages with your comment on their timeline the algorithm will more likely to push your future content to their timeline and then they have a higher probability of retweeting your next tweet for example, that's where a lot of the girls happens I think on Twitter anything that is not text does really well so video screen recordings, js images.So I post a lot of screenshot like for example, if you read a super interesting article literally just let's select the quote you like in this screenshot is that's a good tweet as well cool and so you mentioned earlier posting like three times a day and you started a year and a half ago and now you're at like I don't want to missstate it but like 40,000 followers or something yeah so you just that's an amazing like growth path any other last things to share for someone who's maybe in the 2000 followers receipt that's looking at you it' like okay.and a half I want to be like where she is Yeah I think one just have the discipline of posting consistently even without positive feedback in the beginning is really important just just stick with it and then look at the data what are people engaging with and then once you pick out some patterns that people engage with do more of that and then don't be scared of repeating yourself because I think content is a huge repetition game if you said something that went viral it will go viral the second time.You say the third time you say it will go very over and interesting Okay, so you can have different variations of the same idea.
